title: Anthropic launches Claude design to simplify visual creation with AI
summary: Anthropic launches Claude design to simplify visual creation with AI Advertisement Advertisement Advertisement Anthropic has introduced a new AI-powered design tool called Claude Design, aimed at helping users create visual content such as prototypes, presentations, and marketing assets through
content: Anthropic launches Claude design to simplify visual creation with AI Advertisement Advertisement Advertisement Anthropic has introduced a new AI-powered design tool called Claude Design, aimed at helping users create visual content such as prototypes, presentations, and marketing assets through simple conversational inputs. The product, developed under Anthropic Labs, is currently available in research preview for paid Claude subscribers and is being rolled out gradually. Claude Design is powered by the company’s latest vision model, Claude Opus 4.7, and is positioned as a tool that bridges the gap between technical design expertise and everyday creative needs. ... The core idea behind Claude Design is to simplify the process of creating visual content. Instead of relying on ... Advertisement Advertisement Early feedback highlighted in the announcement suggests that teams can move from concept to working prototypes in a ... For users, Claude Design represents a shift toward more accessible and collaborative creative tools. It allows ... potential use cases across industries. What Comes Next Anthropic has indicated that additional integrations and features will be introduced in the coming weeks, making it easier to connect Claude Design with existing workflows and tools. Advertisement Advertisement As AI continues to reshape creative industries, tools like Claude Design highlight a growing trend toward conversational interfaces that simplify complex tasks. content: OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind, a Specialized Reasoning Model for Drug Discovery and Genomics - The Saudi Times TodayTuesday, April 21, 2026 OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind, a Specialized Reasoning Model for Drug Discovery and Genomics by The Saudi Times 20 April، 2026 Share this OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind, a Specialized Reasoning Model for Drug Discovery and Genomics OpenAI has introduced GPT-Rosalind, a purpose-built reasoning model designed to accelerate pharmaceutical research, genomics analysis, and molecular biology. The model, named after pioneering scientist Rosalind Franklin, is already being integrated by Amgen, Moderna, and Thermo Fisher Scientific as the life sciences sector moves to embed AI more ... General-purpose language models have shown promise in life sciences applications, but the field presents unique ... involved in making claims about biological mechanisms that could eventually inform clinical decisions. GPT-Rosalind was trained on a curated corpus that includes peer-reviewed literature, protein structure databases, genomic datasets, and ... The model is structured around chain-of-thought reasoning capabilities tuned specifically for hypothesis generation and experimental design. In early benchmarks, it demonstrated strong performance on tasks such as predicting gene ... conflicting research. OpenAI said the model is designed to surface its reasoning transparently, a feature that ... Amgen is reported to be using GPT-Rosalind to accelerate target identification in its oncology pipeline. Moderna, which ... The launch of a model at this level of specialization signals a broader shift in how AI companies are approaching high-value verticals. Rather than offering a single general model and expecting enterprise customers to adapt it to their needs, OpenAI is investing in domain-specific training that reduces the customization burden and increases out-of-the-box utility for professional scientific users. For the global life sciences industry, the potential is significant. Drug discovery timelines have historically ... with investments in research infrastructure, biobanking, and genomic medicine. title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design for Creative & Technical Workflow
summary: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design for Creative & Technical Workflow Anthropic Labs unveiled Claude Design, a research-preview product that marks a fundamental shift in how humans interact with Artificial Intelligence. By moving beyond text-based chat into “artifact-based” generation-where the AI prod
content: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design for Creative & Technical Workflow Anthropic Labs unveiled Claude Design, a research-preview product that marks a fundamental shift in how humans interact with Artificial Intelligence. By moving beyond text-based chat into “artifact-based” generation-where the AI produces fully structured, branded, and editable visual assets-Anthropic is signaling that the era of AI-as-a-chatbot is rapidly ending. The new era is one of AI-as-a-creator, capable of generating high-fidelity prototypes, pitch decks, UI mockups, ... Beyond Chat to Artifacts Launched on April 17, 2026, and powered by Anthropic’s flagship Claude Opus 4.7 model, Claude Design is built to function as a “system-aware” collaborator. Unlike generalist image generators that create static, uneditable blobs of pixels, Claude Design reads your company’s codebase, Figma files, and brand assets to ensure that every output is “on-brand” and technically relevant. ... Generation Within Your System: Claude learns your design system (your color palette, fonts, components) so future ... Anthropic has incorporated significant integrations, especially into Canva, which allows ... Also Read: Cadence and NVIDIA Announce Partnership to Reinvent Engineering for the AI and Computing Era Closed-Loop Design-to-Code Engineering: Most importantly for software engineers, Claude Design outputs can be exported in HTML format or transferred to Claude Code. ... Conclusion The release of Claude Design confirms that we are moving toward a future where “creating” is as simple as “describing.” For the IT and DevOps industries, this is a clarion call to modernize the toolchain. title: What is Anthropic Claude Design? Tool launched to rival Figma
summary: What is Anthropic Claude Design? Tool launched to rival Figma - Tech What is Anthropic Claude Design?
content: What is Anthropic Claude Design? Tool launched to rival Figma - Tech What is Anthropic Claude Design? Tool launched to rival Figma Claude Design is powered by Anthropic’s latest Claude Opus 4.7 model. Anthropic has dropped a new AI tool called Claude Design. It can generate polished visual assets ranging from interactive prototypes to pitch decks with just a text prompt. The launch has already rattled the design software market, with Adobe shares dropping 1.and Figma sliding in secondary trading. The announcement comes shortly after Anthropic’s Chief Product Officer resigned from Figma’s board. Claude Design is powered by Anthropic’s latest Claude Opus 4.7 model. It is designed to help both professional designers and non-designers create and refine visual work efficiently. ... Claude Design allows users to start projects by uploading images, documents (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX), or even pointing the AI at an existing codebase. The tool generates a first draft, which can then be refined using inline comments, direct edits, or custom sliders. Anthropic claims that Claude Design can be used for: - Interactive prototypes: Turning static mockups into shareable prototypes for user testing. ... - Frontier design: Creating complex prototypes with voice, video, 3D elements, and shaders. ... 1. Open the Claude app or website. 2. Create a project and upload context (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, screenshots). ... Claude Design also integrates seamlessly with Claude Code, allowing users to hand off completed designs for backend ... Availability Claude Design is available at no extra cost for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers, With this launch, Anthropic is positioning itself as a direct competitor to design platforms like Figma and Adobe. title: xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025
summary: xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025 - The Saudi Times TodayTuesday, April 21, 2026 xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025 by [The Saudi Times](https://thesauditimes.net/en/author/tta
content: xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025 - The Saudi Times TodayTuesday, April 21, 2026 xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025 ... xAI Releases Grok 4.3 Beta with Improved Architecture and Knowledge Updated Through Late 2025 xAI has released Grok 4.3 in beta, an incremental but meaningful update to its flagship conversational AI model. The release brings a revised model architecture, improved reasoning consistency, and a knowledge base extended through December 2025 — addressing one of the most common user complaints about the previous generation, which had a knowledge ... xAI described the architectural changes in Grok 4.3 as refinements to the model’s attention mechanism and training pipeline rather than a wholesale redesign. The result is a model that the company says handles multi-turn conversations with greater coherence, performs more reliably on structured tasks such as data extraction and code generation, and ... The extended knowledge cutoff is a practical improvement that will matter to users who rely on Grok for current-events awareness. Previous Grok versions were trained on data through mid-2024, meaning the model had no direct knowledge of a ... brings Grok significantly closer to the present and makes it more useful for applications where recency is important. The beta release is available to xAI’s existing subscriber base on the X platform, with a broader rollout planned once the company completes its stability and safety evaluations. xAI said it is using the beta period to collect structured ... Grok 4.3 enters a market that has grown considerably more competitive over the past year. OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google have all released new model generations that push the performance frontier, and the gap between the leading labs and challengers has narrowed in some areas while widening in others. xAI’s advantage lies partly in its distribution ... The integration of Grok into X’s social media environment also creates use cases that other AI assistants do not ... that benefit from the model’s access to live data on the platform. Whether Grok 4.3 is enough to close the gap with the current generation of frontier models on standard benchmarks remains to be seen, but xAI’s roadmap suggests further significant releases are expected before the end of the year.
